01 — The Problem

A System Broken
by Chaos.

⚡

Operational Chaos & Low Reliability

Dependence on informal, verbal agreements leads to frequent absences and delays, disrupting students' academic schedules and eroding trust in the service.

👁

Lack of Monitoring & Real-Time Tracking

The traditional system lacks any technical means for students (or parents) to track trips or verify driver credentials, creating significant safety gaps and constant anxiety.

💸

Pricing Inconsistency & Financial Disarray

Students face fluctuating costs and a total absence of structured digital payment systems, making monthly transportation budgeting complex and non-transparent.

📱

University Transport Challenges — Students rely on Facebook groups, Telegram channels, and word-of-mouth to find transport. This creates a fragmented, unaccountable, and unsafe system.

02 — The Solution

The Ecosystem
of Trust.

Integrated Digital Ecosystem for Smart Student Transport — connecting students, verified drivers, and institutions in one structured platform.

🔗

Smart & Reliable Matching

A platform connecting students with a verified database of professional drivers, ensuring a seamless and organized commuting experience.

🛡

Service Continuity Guarantee

A built-in "Backup Driver" system to handle emergencies or absences, ensuring strict adherence to schedules and zero delays.

Unique Feature
📅

Structured Subscriptions

Fixed monthly subscription plans based on optimized routes, ensuring financial transparency for students and income stability for drivers.

📍

Smart Monitoring & Operations

Real-time live tracking and automated daily notifications, providing parents and students with total peace of mind throughout the journey.

✅

Institutional Safety Standards

A rigorous vetting process for drivers and vehicle inspections to ensure the highest levels of safety and professional compliance.

05 — Business Model

Smart Subscriptions for
Sustainable Growth.

Revenue Model (Prepaid Subscriptions)

We operate on a monthly subscription model, securing Monthly Recurring Revenue (MRR) and high cash flow predictability compared to traditional pay-per-ride models.

Pricing Strategy

Fixed monthly tiers based on route distance, providing students with budget clarity and the platform with stable margins.

Driver Partnership

We act as the financial anchor for drivers, providing them with guaranteed monthly income in exchange for strict adherence to routes and safety standards, ensuring high supply-side retention.

Unit Economics — The Magic Numbers

Net Margin / student / month 15K IQD
Lifetime Value (LTV) per student 135K IQD
Customer Acquisition Cost (CAC) 5K IQD
LTV : CAC Ratio 27:1
06 — Traction & Key Metrics

Engineered for
Early Profitability.

🎯

Rapid Break-even Point

The business reaches break-even at only 1,224 active students, significantly lowering initial operational risks.

📈

First-Year Profitability

Financial projections indicate a net operating profit of approximately 82M IQD within the first full year.

💰

Stable & Predictable Cash Flow

Our upfront subscription model ensures consistent monthly recurring revenue (MRR), providing a solid foundation for financial planning and expansion.

Break-even Progress (1,224 students needed) 800 / 1,224
65% of the way there — from organic growth alone
LTV vs CAC chart
07 — Customers & Use Case

Empowering Students,
Supporting Drivers.

🎓

University Students — The End-User

Scenario: A student needing a reliable and comfortable daily commute.
The Need: Punctuality, high safety standards, and transparent, affordable subscription pricing.
Value: Guaranteed seating, real-time trip tracking, and elimination of stress from unverified transport options.
🚗

The Drivers — The Operational Partner

Scenario: A vehicle owner looking to maximize income without searching for individual passengers.
The Need: Guaranteed full occupancy, structured payment collection, minimizing "dead miles."
Value: Structured work schedule, guaranteed upfront earnings, and AI-optimized routing.
08 — Competition & Advantage

Why We Win
Every Time.

Feature Al-Mahatta Ride-Hailing Informal Lines
Fixed Monthly Subscription ✓ ✕ ✕
Backup Driver System ✓ ✕ ✕
Driver Vetting & Safety ✓ ~ ✕
Real-Time Parent Tracking ✓ ~ ✕
Predictable Pricing ✓ ✕ ✕
Student-Specific Routing ✓ ✕ ✕

General Ride-Hailing Apps (e.g., Baly, Karim)

They focus on on-demand, transactional trips with volatile surge pricing, making them unreliable and expensive for daily student commuting.

Traditional Informal Lines

Based on unreliable verbal agreements with zero safety standards or tracking, where a single driver's absence disrupts the student's entire academic day.

Our Unfair Advantage

A stellar 27:1 LTV:CAC ratio driven by high retention and niche-focused marketing, creating a profitable moat that is difficult for mass-market competitors to breach.

09 — The Moat

Why Al-Mahatta
is Built to Last.

🛡

Operational Barrier

Our unique "Backup Driver" system and dedicated student logistics create a level of reliability that general ride-hailing apps cannot replicate without pivoting their entire business model.

🔒

High Switching Costs

Our seasonal subscription model locks in users for the entire academic year. Once a student secures their seat and fixes their budget, the friction of moving back to unreliable alternatives is extremely high.

🎯

Hyper-Targeted Acquisition

While competitors spend heavily on broad marketing, our niche focus on universities allows us to maintain a superior 27:1 LTV:CAC ratio by reaching our audience directly where they aggregate.

📊

Proprietary Routing Data

We are building the first specialized database of student-specific commute patterns in Iraq. This data allows us to continuously optimize route density and margins in a way a generalist platform never could.

🏛

Institutional Trust

We have turned "Safety" from a feature into a moat. By integrating parents into the experience and maintaining rigorous vetting standards, we've built a brand of trust that acts as a powerful barrier against unverified traditional lines.

11 — Roadmap & Milestones

Our Path
to Scale.

Phase 1 — 2026–2027

Market Entry & Dominance

Focus: Launching monthly subscription lines for university students in Basra.
Target: Full operational capacity with 500+ buses and 5,000+ active students.
Goal: Validating the "Backup Driver" system and establishing Al-Mahatta as the trusted brand.

Phase 2 — Late 2027

Service Diversification

Expansion: Launching the "Smart Taxi Service" as an on-demand individual transport feature.
Strategy: Leveraging existing driver network and student data for reliable transport year-round.
Impact: Maximizing platform utility and ensuring year-round revenue streams.

Phase 3 — 2027–2028

Nationwide Scaling

Expansion: Replicating our success in Baghdad — Iraq's largest educational market.
Target: Covering all private and public universities & colleges.
Vision: 20,000+ active subscribers and positioning Al-Mahatta as Iraq's definitive educational transport ecosystem.
